title: The Stonor Letters and Papers, 1290–1483
summary: The repository XML is a U-M-derived version of CME item ACA1723.0001.001, created in 2006 by uncorrected OCR from 460 linked page images of Charles Lethbridge Kingsford's 1919 two-volume edition. Kingsford reconstructed an editorial selection from dispersed Public Record Office records, chiefly Ancient Correspondence now in TNA SC 1, with selected Chancery Miscellanea now in C 47, Ancient Deeds now in C 146, a Ministers' Account now in SC 6, and an Early Chancery Proceedings item now cataloged as C 1/31/534. The electronic text retains the 333 numbered documents and Kingsford's headnotes but omits most print introduction, appendices, glossary, and indexes. A 1924 supplement, Carpenter's 1996 reissue, and later-recovered C 47/37/22 and /23 material are recorded as later layers, not sources of the 1919 edition or CME encoding.
review_notes: The cataloged work is Kingsford's editorially assembled selection, not a single authored medieval work and not the complete historical Stonor archive.
The XML header's DA 20 .R9 Ser.3 v.29-30 is a library call number for the print series, not an archival shelfmark.
Historical Public Record Office designations and modern TNA shelfmarks are preserved separately; current catalog dates do not replace Kingsford's conjectural document dates.
Later-recovered C 47/37/22 and C 47/37/23 material is relevant for comparison but is not placed upstream of 1919, 1924, or CME without evidence of use.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for aca1723
entities.description: Repository XML supplied through the pinned CME submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item ACA1723.0001.001
entities.description: A 2006 Level 1 TEI-in-Libraries encoding created by uncorrected OCR. It links 460 page images, encodes the 333 numbered documents and Kingsford's headnotes, and represents most front and back matter only by page milestones and omitted-matter wrappers.

entities.label: U-M 460-image scan set for ACA1723.0001.001
entities.description: The exact page-image set named by the CME header and PB milestones. The publicly downloadable U-M package contains 460 TIFF files matching the electronic item's image references, plus pageview data, OCR text, header data, and an earlier XML export.
entities.reproduction_format: 460 monochrome 600 dpi TIFF G4 page images in a downloadable ZIP package

entities.label: Kingsford's 1919 Stonor Letters and Papers
entities.description: The immediate two-volume source edition. Kingsford selected and arranged 333 numbered units from a dispersed documentary archive, printed almost all identified letters, gave full copies or abstracts of selected deeds and other records, added a few illustrative documents outside the historical Stonor archive, and supplied source notes, editorial dates, introduction, calendars, glossary, and indexes.

bibliographic.citation: The Stonor Letters and Papers, 1290–1483, ed. Charles Lethbridge Kingsford, 2 vols., Camden Third Series 29–30 (London: Offices of the Royal Historical Society, 1919).
bibliographic.title: The Stonor Letters and Papers, 1290–1483
bibliographic.publisher: Offices of the Royal Historical Society

entities.label: PIMS/Internet Archive scans of Kingsford's 1919 volumes
entities.description: An independent two-volume scan useful for checking the U-M OCR and omitted print matter; it is a facsimile of the printed edition, not of the medieval archival documents.
entities.reproduction_format: Two 400 ppi scanned volumes; 240 and 246 scan pages, including two foldouts in volume I

entities.label: Selected Stonor correspondence in TNA SC 1
entities.description: The principal archival source group for Kingsford's letters, especially current SC 1/46, with additional Stonor material in SC 1/44, /51, /52, and /60. SC 1 is an archival series and this entity represents only the identified Stonor components, not the whole series.

holding.shelfmark: SC 1/44, SC 1/46, SC 1/51, SC 1/52, and SC 1/60 (selected items)
holding.notes: No single catalog record represents this cross-series selection; the exact SC 1/46 subgroup and representative first and last Kingsford items have direct current records.

entities.label: Selected Stonor miscellanea in TNA C 47/37
entities.description: The historical Chancery Miscellanea 37 records from which Kingsford printed or abstracted selected documents, including shrieval papers, household accounts, inventories, and other Stonor material. This aggregate excludes later-recovered C 47/37/22 and /23 as upstream sources unless separately evidenced.

holding.notes: The entity aggregates selected components. Direct current component records, rather than the generic C 47 series record, support the holding.

entities.label: Selected Stonor deeds in TNA C 146
entities.description: The deeds historically cited by Kingsford as Ancient Deeds, class C, from which eleven numbered units in the CME text are directly sourced and additional deeds are calendared in the print appendix.

holding.shelfmark: C 146/221 and other selected C 146 items
holding.notes: C 146/221 is the current exact record for Kingsford item 1; the entity groups additional deeds whose complete item-level crosswalk remains to be made.

entities.label: TNA SC 6/1250/6, rents of Sir William Stonor
entities.description: The Ministers' Account from which Kingsford item 228 gives an abstract of rents at Michaelmas 1478.

entities.label: TNA C 1/31/534, Frende v Fortescue
entities.description: The Early Chancery Proceedings record matching Kingsford item 80, a complaint by John Frende against Richard Fortescue concerning false imprisonment in Devon. Kingsford prints the historical source designation as 31/34–36; the current exact Discovery record is C 1/31/534.

date_statements.label: current catalog date
date_statements.notes: This preserves TNA's alternative ranges. Kingsford instead assigns item 80 to 1466 from related King's Bench proceedings.

holding.notes: The title and parties establish the modern match; the reason for the difference between Kingsford's printed number and the current reference is unresolved.

entities.label: Supplementary Stonor Letters and Papers (1314–1482)
entities.description: Kingsford's later supplement in Camden Third Series 34. It is later scholarship and is not an upstream source for the 1919 edition or U-M encoding.

bibliographic.citation: Charles Lethbridge Kingsford, ‘Supplementary Stonor Letters and Papers (1314–1482),’ Camden Third Series 34 (1924), 1–26, doi:10.1017/S2042171000006907.
bibliographic.title: Supplementary Stonor Letters and Papers (1314–1482)

entities.label: Carpenter's 1996 Kingsford's Stonor Letters and Papers
entities.description: A one-volume reissue of Kingsford's 1919 volumes and 1924 supplement, with Christine Carpenter's new introduction, annotation, and supplementary listing of later-found records.

bibliographic.citation: Kingsford's Stonor Letters and Papers 1290–1483, ed. Christine Carpenter (Cambridge: Cambridge University Press, 1996).
bibliographic.title: Kingsford's Stonor Letters and Papers 1290–1483

entities.label: TNA C 47/37/22, later-listed Stonor material
entities.description: A current item-level group of Stonor records, including material recovered or added after Kingsford's edition. Carpenter listed later-found material in 1996; Noble reports 152 documents or fragments, while the current catalog has a parent record and item/component numbering that requires reconciliation.

entities.label: TNA C 47/37/23, Stonor household account book
entities.description: A 24-folio household account book, chiefly provisions and miscellaneous expenses, cataloged as added in June 1998. It postdates Carpenter's research and is not a source for the 1919 or 1996 editions.

holding.notes: The current catalog states that this component was added in June 1998.

scope.notes: The repository file self-identifies as the U-M item; byte identity with every historical U-M export is not asserted.

editorial_practices.description: Kingsford attempted to reconstruct the dispersed Stonor archive, printed all identified letters except fragments he judged too slight, gave full texts or abstracts of deeds and other records selected for interest, calendared further material, and added a few illustrative documents he expressly said did not belong to the archive.
editorial_practices.scope: The two-volume edited collection, including numbered documents and appendices

editorial_practices.representation: Expanded forms with stated exceptions; modern u/v and i/j usage; thorn and yogh generally retained
editorial_practices.description: Kingsford says original spelling is retained, with limited expansion of abbreviated forms such as with and that, modernized distribution of u/v and i/j subject to characteristic exceptions, preservation of signature spelling, and general retention of thorn and yogh.

Documents are arranged as nearly chronologically as Kingsford considered possible; letters of uncertain date concerning the same subject may be grouped together instead of placed by a strict inferred date.

The electronic text encodes the 333 numbered documents and Kingsford's prefatory notes but omits most textual front matter and all appendix, glossary, and index text; page milestones and linked images still represent those omitted pages.
notes: The header states that OCR was not corrected and content was not edited; the encoding should not be treated as a diplomatic transcript of the archival documents or a complete electronic edition of all 1919 print matter.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/aca1723.xml.
evidence.summary: Identifies U-M item ACA1723.0001.001, Kingsford's 1919 two-volume source, 460 linked TIFF images, uncorrected OCR and TEI Level 1 encoding, generic availability terms, omitted front/back matter, 333 numbered documents, historical source citations, and Kingsford's headnotes and dates.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, CME item ACA1723.0001.001, The Stonor Letters and Papers, 1290–1483.
evidence.summary: Confirms the stable item, public digital text, Kingsford 1919 source, page-image links, subjects, and U-M's current Public Domain/No Copyright–United States assertion.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive, U-M raw package for aca1723.0001.001.umich.edu.
evidence.summary: The anonymously retrievable ZIP was inspected and contains exactly 460 TIFF files matching the XML page references, plus pageview data, OCR text, header data, and an earlier U-M XML export. Item metadata identifies U-M provenance and provides no item-specific license or rights field.
notes: The embedded older XML export differs bytewise from the repository XML through declaration, identifiers, formatting, and header changes; content-level derivation is supported, but exact byte identity is not asserted.

evidence.citation: Charles Lethbridge Kingsford, ed., The Stonor Letters and Papers, 1290–1483, vols. I–II (1919), PIMS/Internet Archive scans.
evidence.locator: Title pages; vol. I pp. i–lvi and 1–165; vol. II pp. 1–224; appendices pp. 165–191; glossary and indexes pp. 192–224
evidence.summary: Complete independent scans confirm publication and series data, two-volume pagination, numbered sequence 1–333, document texts and headnotes, introduction, appendices and calendars, glossary, and indexes. Item metadata identifies PIMS/U-Toronto digitization at 400 ppi in 2011 and provides no item-specific rights/license field.

evidence.citation: Kingsford, introduction, The Stonor Letters and Papers, vol. I (1919), pp. xxxvi–xlvii.
evidence.locator: vol. I, pp. xxxvi–xxxviii and xlvii
evidence.summary: Kingsford reconstructs the papers' archival dispersal, identifies Ancient Correspondence, Ancient Deeds, Ministers' Accounts, and Chancery Miscellanea, states his selection of almost all letters plus full texts or abstracts and calendars of other records, identifies a few added illustrative documents, describes chronological arrangement, and states spelling, abbreviation, u/v, i/j, thorn, and yogh practices. He counts 256 letters excluding fragments and more than 600 historical documents overall.

evidence.citation: Royal Historical Society, Camden Third Series publications list.
evidence.summary: Lists The Stonor Letters and Papers as Camden Third Series volumes XXIX and XXX (1919) and Supplementary Stonor Letters and Papers as volume XXXIV (1924).

evidence.citation: The National Archives Discovery, current records for the archival classes and exact Stonor components cited by Kingsford.
evidence.summary: Supports the current SC 1, C 47, C 146, SC 6, and C 1 identifiers used in this manifest and demonstrates why Kingsford's historical designations must be retained separately rather than mechanically normalized.

evidence.citation: The National Archives Discovery, SC 1/46 and representative Stonor items SC 1/46/1 and SC 1/46/102.
evidence.summary: The current SC 1/46 subgroup is described at item level. Exact records identify John de Stonor's Valladolid letter of 11 April 1325 as SC 1/46/1 and Francis Lovell's 11 October 1483 letter to William Stonor as SC 1/46/102, matching Kingsford's historical A.C. xlvi references and collection endpoints.

evidence.citation: The National Archives Discovery, C 47/37/1 and C 47/37/2.
evidence.summary: Current C 47 component records identify Stonor shrieval and estate records and Alice de Stonor's household account book within Chancery Miscellanea. The generic C 47 series history explains its formation by rearrangement of miscellaneous records; exact components support the selected holding.

evidence.citation: The National Archives Discovery, C 146/221, grant by Richard de Stonor.
evidence.summary: The exact current record matches Kingsford item 1 and historical Ancient Deeds C. 221, but dates it broadly 1200–1300 rather than accepting Kingsford's c. 1290 without qualification. The C 146 series record describes the Ancient Deeds, Series C arrangement.

evidence.citation: The National Archives Discovery, SC 6/1250/6.
evidence.summary: Exact record for William Stonour's Watlington lands and arrears, dated 18 Edward IV, matching Kingsford item 228's historical Ministers Accounts 1250/6 citation.

evidence.citation: The National Archives Discovery, C 1/31/534, Frende v Fortescue.
evidence.summary: The exact current record names John Frende and Richard Fortescue, concerns false imprisonment in Devon, and gives the alternative date ranges 1465–1471 or perhaps 1480–1483, establishing the match to Kingsford item 80 despite his printed historical citation Early Chancery Proceedings 31/34–36 and his editorial date 1466.

evidence.citation: The National Archives Discovery, C 47/37/22 and C 47/37/23.
evidence.summary: The /22 parent is described at item level; current numbering reaches at least /157 and includes verso component records. The /23 record describes a 24-folio household account book and states that it was added in June 1998, after Carpenter's 1996 edition.

evidence.citation: The National Archives, booking a visit to view documents and reader registration.
evidence.summary: Provides current routes and requirements for obtaining a reader ticket, booking, and consulting original records onsite.

evidence.citation: The National Archives, record copying service and fees.
evidence.summary: Explains page checks and mediated digital-copy orders. On 11 July 2026 the published fees were £9.92 for a page check, £1.52 per digital image up to A3, and £11.95 per image over A3; fees can change and do not grant publication rights.

evidence.citation: The National Archives, Copyright and re-use of materials.
evidence.summary: Distinguishes Open Government Licence reuse of eligible written content and transcriptions from supplied record images. Ordered digital copies are limited to private non-commercial research and education, while publication of record images requires Image Library permission and may incur fees; TNA and the document reference should be credited.

evidence.citation: Cambridge Core, Charles Lethbridge Kingsford, Supplementary Stonor Letters and Papers (1314–1482).
evidence.summary: Confirms title, author, Camden Third Series volume 34, December 1924 publication, pages 1–26, DOI, current Cambridge platform access, and later online publication date.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Press and Google Books, Kingsford's Stonor Letters and Papers 1290–1483, ed. Christine Carpenter (1996).
evidence.summary: Confirms the 1996 Cambridge publication, Carpenter's editorship, ISBNs, 519-page extent, and reissue of Kingsford's three parts with a new introduction, annotation, and supplementary listing.

evidence.citation: Elizabeth Noble, The World of the Stonors: A Gentry Society (Woodbridge: Boydell, 2009), publisher page and preview.
evidence.summary: Noble distinguishes letters from the larger documentary archive, reports Kingsford's estimate of about 600 documents and 256 letters excluding fragments, describes 152 later-found documents or fragments listed by Carpenter, and notes the separately added C 47/37/23 household book after 1996.

evidence.citation: Alison Truelove, ‘Linguistic Diversity in the Fifteenth-Century Stonor Letters,’ Reading Medieval Studies 31 (2005).
evidence.summary: States that Kingsford's transcriptions are not consistently accurate and that the study used revised transcriptions, supporting caution about treating the 1919 text and uncorrected U-M OCR as diplomatic evidence for manuscript detail.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.
evidence.summary: Supports the U.S. term assessment for the 1919 and 1924 publications and the continued protection of new 1996 scholarly matter.

open_questions.question: What is the exact current TNA reference and representation type for every source document in Kingsford's 333 numbered units and appendices?
next_steps: Create an item-level table from each Kingsford headnote and appendix entry to current Discovery records.
Record whether each source is printed in full, excerpted, abstracted, merely cited, grouped with another document, or added from outside the historical Stonor archive.
Confirm current references directly with TNA where catalog rearrangement prevents a mechanical crosswalk.

open_questions.question: Why does Kingsford cite Early Chancery Proceedings 31/34–36 for John Frende v. Richard Fortescue while the exact current TNA record is C 1/31/534?
next_steps: Consult historical Early Chancery Proceedings finding aids and TNA transfer/renumbering documentation.
Ask TNA whether 31/34–36 was a legacy range, a typesetting error, or a designation for multiple membranes now cataloged under C 1/31/534.

open_questions.question: How do Carpenter's and Noble's 152 later-found documents or fragments correspond to the current C 47/37/22 hierarchy, whose numbered items reach at least 157 and include verso component records?
next_steps: Compare Carpenter's supplementary listing item by item with the current Discovery export.
Distinguish physical documents, fragments, catalog items, and separately described recto/verso components.
Keep C 47/37/23 separate because it was added in 1998 and was unavailable to Carpenter's 1996 edition.

open_questions.question: What reuse terms govern the exact U-M raw TIFF package and the independent PIMS/Internet Archive scan derivatives?
next_steps: Obtain written clarification from U-M and PIMS/Internet Archive before redistributing their page-image files.
Rely on separately generated text or a clearly licensed source for publication workflows unless permission is confirmed.
Keep the public-domain status of the 1919 content separate from provider terms for modern digital files.

open_questions.question: Is a complete scholarly edition or accessible corpus of Alison Truelove's revised Stonor transcriptions available for systematic comparison?
next_steps: Consult Truelove's 2001 thesis and subsequent publication history.
Ask the author or repository about access to complete revised transcriptions.
Treat Kingsford's readings and U-M's uncorrected OCR as fallible when researching manuscript letterforms, abbreviations, or exact wording.
